configurationStatus: Needs attention — 2 issues found. Your Stripe webhook configuration is mostly correct but has a critical missing signature verification and a warning about the endpoint URL.

issues.description: Webhook signature verification is not configured. Incoming webhook events are not validated, leaving the endpoint vulnerable to spoofed requests.
issues.fix: Add Stripe webhook signature verification using your webhook signing secret: StripeWebhookSignatures.VerifyHeader(payload, sigHeader, webhookSecret)

issues.description: The webhook endpoint URL uses HTTP instead of HTTPS. Stripe requires HTTPS for production endpoints.
issues.fix: Update your endpoint URL to use HTTPS. If running locally, use a tool like ngrok to create an HTTPS tunnel.

issues.description: Consider adding idempotency handling for webhook events to prevent duplicate processing.
issues.fix: Store processed event IDs in a database and check before processing new events.

recommendations: Enable Stripe's test mode webhook events to verify your handler works correctly before going live
Add structured logging for all webhook events to aid debugging
Set up monitoring alerts for webhook failures
nextStepSuggestion: Use the Error Explainer if you encounter any errors during webhook setup.
nextStepDescription: If you encounter errors while implementing these fixes, the Error Explainer can analyse the stack trace and provide specific guidance for your Stripe webhook configuration.
